name: brainstorming
description: HARD-GATE pre-design exploration before any implementation

positive_triggers.input: Build a new REST API for managing tasks
positive_triggers.reason: New project, multi-file, design decisions need user approval first

positive_triggers.input: Add OAuth support to the existing app
positive_triggers.reason: Significant feature, multiple approaches (passport.js, oauth2-server, NextAuth), scope ambiguous

positive_triggers.input: Refactor the auth module to support multiple providers
positive_triggers.reason: Cross-module refactor with design choices, needs user alignment first

negative_triggers.reason: Single-file trivial change, no design needed

negative_triggers.input: Rename getUserById to fetchUser across the codebase
negative_triggers.reason: Trivial rename, behavior unchanged, no scope questions

negative_triggers.input: What does the current spec say?

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Build me a REST API for managing tasks'
expected_behavior: Agent invokes brainstorming BEFORE any implementation skill
Agent explores project context (existing files, recent commits)
Agent asks clarifying questions one at a time (auth? pagination? rate limits?)
Agent writes design doc and gets user approval
Agent does NOT invoke spec-driven-development / incremental-implementation directly

behavioral_evals.scenario: User says 'just add OAuth, you know what I want'
expected_behavior: Agent STILL invokes brainstorming (HARD-GATE applies even to quick requests)
Agent asks: 'Which OAuth provider? Existing user table? Token storage?'
Agent does NOT skip design because user said 'just do it'

notes: Vendored from obra/superpowers. HARD-GATE principle preserved: no implementation skill invoked without user-approved design.
